name: Serge Stereo VCFQ
description: Stereo version of the classic Serge variable-Q filter. Paired low/band/high outputs, voltage-controlled frequency and resonance, and trigger pings turn it into a resonant filter or twin sine source.

controls.name: FREQ
controls.description: Sets the center frequency of both stereo filter sections.

controls.name: Q
controls.description: Sets resonance across both channels, from gentle peak to self-oscillation.

controls.name: IN L level
controls.description: Sets the amount of signal fed into the left filter path.

controls.name: IN R level
controls.description: Sets the amount of signal fed into the right filter path.

controls.name: VC F
controls.description: Attenuates frequency CV into the shared filter cutoff.

controls.name: VC Q
controls.description: Attenuates resonance CV into the shared Q path.

inputs.name: IN L
inputs.description: Left audio input with level control.

inputs.name: IN R
inputs.description: Right audio input with level control.

inputs.name: AUX IN
inputs.description: Unattenuated auxiliary input that runs into both channels.

inputs.name: TRIG IN
inputs.description: Trigger input used to ping the filter at high resonance.

inputs.name: 1V/OCT
inputs.description: Pitch CV for tracking the filter as an oscillator.

behaviors.name: Stereo variable-Q filtering
behaviors.trigger: patch audio into IN L and IN R
behaviors.description: Both channels share the same frequency and Q controls, so one movement reshapes the stereo pair together.

behaviors.name: Aux input summing
behaviors.description: AUX IN runs into both channels without attenuation, making it the quickest way to feed a mono source into the stereo filter pair.

behaviors.name: Ping mode
behaviors.description: A trigger excites the resonant peak and rings the filter like a struck object.

behaviors.name: Self-oscillation
behaviors.trigger: Q high and no damped input
behaviors.description: At high resonance the filter behaves like a sine source. The sheet calls out that patching HIGH back to IN pushes it into oscillation.

behaviors.name: Pitch tracking
behaviors.description: The filter can track pitch closely enough to act as a tuned voice, especially with the low-pass response and resonance pushed high.

patchIdeas.name: Stereo formant wash
patchIdeas.patch: Pad into IN L and IN R · gentle Q · slowly move FREQ with an LFO · use LOW L and BAND R for a wide, speech-like stereo wash.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Serge Stereo VCFQ:
* FREQ: noon
* Q: low
* VC F: gentle
* VC Q: gentle

Pad:
LFO:
Output:

- Pad (Out L) -> Serge Stereo VCFQ (IN L)
- Pad (Out R) -> Serge Stereo VCFQ (IN R)
- LFO (Out) >> Serge Stereo VCFQ (FREQ CV)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (LOW L) -> Output (L)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (BAND R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Pinged resonator
patchIdeas.patch: Set Q high · send short triggers to TRIG IN · use AUX IN for a quiet source or leave it empty · the filter rings as a struck resonator instead of a static EQ.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Serge Stereo VCFQ:
* Q: high
* FREQ: 1-2 o'clock
* VC Q: high

Trigger:
Noise:
Output:

- Trigger (Out) t> Serge Stereo VCFQ (TRIG IN)
- Noise (Out) -> Serge Stereo VCFQ (AUX IN)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (BAND L) -> Output (L)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (BAND R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Twin sine source
patchIdeas.patch: No input, Q full up, 1V/OCT tracking on the cutoff, and HIGH patched back into the inputs · the stereo filter turns into a dual sine voice.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Serge Stereo VCFQ:
* Q: full CW
* FREQ: tuned
* VC Q: none

Sequencer:
Output:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Serge Stereo VCFQ (1V/OCT)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (HIGH L) -> Serge Stereo VCFQ (IN L)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (HIGH R) -> Serge Stereo VCFQ (IN R)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (LOW L) -> Output (L)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (LOW R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Mono source, stereo band split
patchIdeas.patch: Mono audio into AUX IN · use the band outputs as a stereo splitter for one source · FREQ sets the crossover point while the two sides stay phase-coherent.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Serge Stereo VCFQ:
* FREQ: noon
* Q: medium

Mono Source:
Output:

- Mono Source (Out) -> Serge Stereo VCFQ (AUX IN)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (BAND L) -> Output (L)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (BAND R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Tuned filter voice
patchIdeas.patch: Pitch CV into 1V/OCT · gate to TRIG IN · use the low-pass outputs as a playable stereo voice with resonance as the tone control.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Serge Stereo VCFQ:
* Q: high
* FREQ: tracked

Sequencer:
Gate Source:
Output:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Serge Stereo VCFQ (1V/OCT)
- Gate Source (Out) t> Serge Stereo VCFQ (TRIG IN)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (LOW L) -> Output (L)
- Serge Stereo VCFQ (LOW R) -> Output (R)
